nodes.context: Chinese American youth gang active in San Francisco's Chinatown, involved in drug trafficking, extortion, murder.
Youth gang formed in 1964 to protect new Chinese immigrants; later absorbed into Hop Sing Tong.
Traditional Chinatown organization that absorbed the Wah Ching as its youth wing.
Splinter group from Wah Ching formed in 1969 by members unhappy with merger into Hop Sing Tong.
Traditional Chinatown organization rival to Hop Sing Tong; involved in conflict with Wah Ching.

edges.context: The Joe Boys and Wah Ching were engaged in a violent war from 1973, culminating in the Golden Dragon Massacre.
The infobox lists Suey Sing Tong as an ally of the Joe Boys.
The infobox lists Hop Sing Tong as a rival of the Joe Boys.
The Wah Ching were absorbed into the Hop Sing Tong around 1968.
The Yau Lai split from the Wah Ching in 1969 due to dissatisfaction with the merger into Hop Sing Tong.
Joe Fong's group splintered from the Yau Lai in early 1971 and later re-formed as the Joe Boys.
The Suey Sing Tong fought with the Wah Ching, leading to the killing of Joe Fong's brother and subsequent retaliation.
